title: Add USA Prefix to Supported Salesforce Instance Locations
description:
## Context
Salesforce orgs provisioned in the US may have instance keys prefixed with either "NA" or "USA". The location middleware only recognized "NA" prefixes, blocking users with "USA" prefixed instances.

## Implementation
The middleware resolves the instance key prefix by querying the Salesforce Status API (`/v1/instanceAliases/{orgAlias}`) and comparing the prefix against the location lookup table. The fix adds "USA" as a supported prefix that maps to the "NA" region, allowing those users through.

## Technical Detail
- Instance key is extracted from the `SalesforceUserInformation` model (1:1 with team account)
- Prefix comparison uses the `key` column in the location lookup model
- If the resolved location is "NA", the user is allowed access; otherwise they're redirected to a "not allowed" page
acceptance_criteria: Users with USA-prefixed Salesforce instance keys can authenticate successfully
Non-NA/USA prefixed instances are correctly redirected to the not-allowed page
middleware_changes: Updated location middleware to recognize USA prefix as a valid NA region identifier when checking Salesforce instance keys.
data_model_changes: Added USA prefix entry to the location lookup table mapping to the NA region.
implementation_notes: The middleware queries the Salesforce Status API for the instance alias, extracts the prefix, and validates it against the location lookup table. USA prefix now maps to NA region.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the solution and deployed to production. Explained the middleware logic for instance key prefix validation. Reporter confirmed it works.
